message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The drastic increase in the number of coronaviruses discovered and coronavirus genomes being sequenced have given us a great opportunity to perform genomics and bioinformatics analysis on this family of viruses. Coronaviruses possess the largest genomes (26.4 to 31.7 kb) among all known RNA viruses, with G + C contents varying from 32% to 43%. Phylogenetically, three genera, Alphacoronavirus, Betacoronavirus and Gammacoronavirus, with Betacoronavirus consisting of subgroups A, B, C were known to exist but now a new genus D also exists,namely the Deltacoronavirus. In such a situation, it becomes highly important for efficient classification of all virus data so that it helps us in suitable planning,containment and treatment. The objective of this paper is to classify SARS corona-virus nucleotide sequences based on parameters such as
                  <jats:bold>sequence length,percentage similarity between the sequence information,open and closed gaps in the sequence due to multiple mutations</jats:bold>
                  and many others.By doing this,we will be able to predict accurately the similarity of
                  <jats:bold>SARS CoV-2</jats:bold>
                  virus with respect to other corona-viruses like the Wuhan corona-virus,the bat corona-virus and the pneumonia virus and would help us better understand about the
                  <jats:bold>taxonomy</jats:bold>
                  of the corona-virus family.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>SUMMARY</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    In addition to the guidelines provided in the abstract above,the following points summarizes the article below:
                    <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>The article discusses an application of Machine Learning in the field of virology.</jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>It aims to classify the SARS CoV2 virus as per the already known sequences of the bat-coronavirus, the Wuhan Sea Food Market pneumonia virus and the Wuhan coronavirus.</jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>
                          To solve and predict the similarity of the SARS CoV2 coronavirus w.r.t other viruses discussed above,
                          <jats:bold>K-Means Unsupervised Learning</jats:bold>
                          Algorithm has been chosen.
                        </jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>
                          The data-set used is
                          <jats:bold>MN997409.1-4NY0T82X016-Alignment-HitTable.csv</jats:bold>
                          found on
                          <jats:bold>
                            <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="http://www.kaggle.com">www.kaggle.com</jats:ext-link>
                          </jats:bold>
                          .(Complete link shared in the references section).
                          <jats:bold>[17]</jats:bold>
                        </jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>
                          The results have been validated by using a simple data-correlation technique namely
                          <jats:bold>Spearman’s Rank Correlation Coeffecient</jats:bold>
                          .
                        </jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                      <jats:list-item>
                        <jats:p>
                          I have also discussed my future work using
                          <jats:bold>Deep Neural Nets</jats:bold>
                          that can help predict new virus sequences and effectively find similarity if any with already discovered viruses.
                        </jats:p>
                      </jats:list-item>
                    </jats:list>
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Genomic Similarity of Nucleotides in SARS CoronaVirus using K-Means Unsupervised Learning Algorithm

reference.unstructured: de Groot RJ , Baker SC , Baric R , Enjuanes L , Gorbalenya AE , Holmes KV , et al. Family Coronaviridae. In: King AMQ , Adams MJ , Carstens EB , Lefkowitz EJ , editors. Virus taxonomy. Ninth report of the international committee on taxonomy of viruses, Elsevier Academic Press; 2012. pp. 806–828.

reference.article-title: Molecular epidemiology, evolution and phylogeny of SARS coronavirus

reference.unstructured: Alagaili AN , Briese T , Mishra N , Kapoor V , Sameroff SC , de Wit E , et al. Middle east respiratory syndrome coronavirus infection in dromedary camels in Saudi Arabia. MBio. 2014; 5. https://doi.org/10.1128/mBio.00884-14

Gurjit S. Randhawa ID1 , Maximillian P. M. Soltysiak ID2 , Hadi El Roz ID2 Camila P. E. Souza , Kathleen A. Hill 2 , Lila Kari Machine learning using intrinsic genomic signatures for rapid classification of novel pathogens: COVID-19 case study.https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0232391
